title: net/http Transport Ownership, Redirects, Bodies, and Timeouts · Go Build Room
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds owned bounded net/http retrieval adapter from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of owned bounded net/http retrieval adapter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Configure and reuse one owned Transport with explicit proxy, dial, TLS, idle pool, compression, limits, and CloseIdleConnections policy.
Build safe retrieval requests with context, honest User-Agent and contact, Accept, validators, method, credentials, and trace identity.
Use CheckRedirect to reapply authorization, URL, credentials, method, DNS, loop, and hop budgets while retaining the chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport Reuse
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6736 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove Transport reuse, request identity, redirect checks, body limits, deadlines, and closure; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport Reuse.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for configure and reuse one owned transport with explicit proxy, dial, tls, idle pool, compression, limits, and closeidleconnections policy. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport Reuse, keep Transport Reuse connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
